title: The Perils of Centralizing Global Environmetal Governance
summary: Juma argues that a new global environmental organization will not replace existing efforts but will add another layer of bureaucracy to an already complex network of treaties and organizations. There is no time to waste on launching a costly, politically divisive and bureaucratic structure.
body: Abstract Juma argues that a new global environmental organization will not replace existing efforts but will add another layer of bureaucracy to an already complex network of treaties and organizations. There is no time to waste on launching a costly, politically divisive and bureaucratic structure. To view full text please see PDF below (login may be required).
